title: 2025-2026 Fall Semester Course Registration Procedures
content:
2025-2026 Fall Semester Course Registration Procedures

2025-2026 Fall Semester Course Registration Procedures

Course Registration Procedures

For the Fall Semester of the 2025–2026 Academic Year, course registration for Graduate, Undergraduate, and Associate Degree students will take place between

September 29 – October 12, 2025

.

The

add/drop period

October 13 – 19, 2025

. (Course selections can also be made during this period.)

For

first-year students

, course registration will be completed automatically. They do not need to take any action regarding course registration. They are only required to log in to the Student Information System and check that their courses have been assigned.

Procedure

1-

Students will select the courses they will take this semester via the course selection screen in the

Student Information System (OBS)

.

2-

Students will confirm their course registration online and submit it for their advisor’s online approval.

3-

Students who wish to consult their advisor regarding course registration may contact them via messaging through the

STIX

system or by email.

For questions related to course registration procedures, please contact your advisor first.

Student advising procedures will be carried out in accordance with the provisions of our University’s

Student Advising Directive

.

Important Notes for Undergraduate and Associate Degree Students

Course registration for 1

st

-year students will be completed automatically. They do not need to take any action regarding course registration. They are only required to log in to the

Student Information System (OBS)

to check that their courses have been assigned.

Only 2

nd

, 3

rd

, and 4

th

-year students are required to complete course registration through the

Student Information System (OBS)

.

Students must first register for courses they previously failed or have not yet taken from lower semesters. During registration, the chronological order of lower semesters must be followed. Courses removed from or no longer offered in the curriculum will be replaced by designated alternative courses.

The regular semester course load is 30 ECTS, and the maximum course load is 45 ECTS.

Students cannot take courses that have not been approved by their academic advisors.

With advisor approval, students may retake courses they have previously passed to improve their grade. In such cases, the most recent grade will be valid, regardless of the previous grade.

Students will complete course registration via the

Student Information System (OBS)

and submit it electronically for advisor approval. Once approved by the advisor through the system, the course registration process will be finalized.

Students whose course registrations are not approved by their advisors will not be allowed to attend classes.

Student status will become active on e-Government (e-Devlet) only after advisor approval of course registration.

Students who wish to consult their advisors regarding course registration may contact them via the

STIX

messaging system or through their email addresses.

1

st

-year students

are not allowed to take courses from upper semesters.

Double Major and Minor students may complete their major course registration first and then select their double major or minor program courses through the

Student Information System (OBS)

.

Students who have successfully completed all courses from previous semesters and have a GPA of 3.00 or above may, starting from the third semester, take one course from an upper year with advisor approval. Students with a GPA of 3.50 or above may take up to two courses from an upper year.

Students who have completed the 2

nd

year of Associate Degree programs or the 4th year of Undergraduate programs but still have outstanding courses will complete course registration through the course registration form submitted to the Registrar’s Office.

Undergraduate students whose GPA falls below 1.80 will be placed on probationary status. At the end of the 4th semester, students who are still on probation can only take a limited number of courses until they return to good standing:

With a GPA of 1.60–1.69, a maximum of 2 courses.

With a GPA of 1.70–1.79, a maximum of 3 courses.

Courses Offered for Our International Students

TURK113 Turkish Language I

(Instructor Selçuk Duman –Medium of Instruction: Turkish, content adjusted according to students’ level)

ATA113 Principles of Atatürk and History of Revolutions I

(Instructor Selçuk Duman – Medium of Instruction: English, content adjusted according to students’ level)

International students who wish to take the Turkish Language and Principles of Atatürk and History of Revolutions courses in Turkish via Distance Education may apply to their academic advisor or the respective course instructor to request a course change.

TURK103 Turkish Language I –Instructor Selçuk Duman

ATA103 Principles of Atatürk and History of Revolutions I – Instructor Selçuk Duman

Important Notes for Graduate Students

Students in the scientific preparation stage are required to select the scientific preparation courses listed in the curriculum.

The semester course load for students in the course stage is 30 ECTS.

Students in the thesis stage must register for the Thesis course. (Students in their 5

th

and 6

th

semesters may extend their studies by paying an extension fee to the Financial Affairs Office and completing their course registration.)

Students cannot take courses that have not been approved by their academic advisors.

Course registration must be completed via the

Student Information System (OBS)

and submitted electronically for advisor approval. Once the advisor approves through the system, the course registration process will be finalized.

Students whose course registrations are not approved by their advisors will not be allowed to attend classes.

Student status will become active on e-Government (e-Devlet) only after advisor approval of course registration.

Students who wish to consult their advisor regarding course registration may contact them via the

STIX

messaging system

or through their email addresses.
